title: limeum aethiopicum
intro: Limeum aethiopicum, also known as Abyssinian wormseed, is a plant species that belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae, characterized by its small, yellowish-green flowers and branching stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to combat parasitic infections, such as hookworms and roundworms, due to the presence of alkaloids and other compounds. The health benefits of Limeum aethiopicum include its antihelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of parasitic infections and reduce inflammation in the body. Limeum aethiopicum is often used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures and decoctions, to treat various health conditions, including parasitic infections and gastrointestinal disorders.
